items.description: Minor abrasion and paper fiber protrusion at the extreme bottom-left corner, with slight material loss.
items.scholarly_note: Typical mechanical edge wear in a frequently handled quarto codex.

items.description: Series of small dark punctures and localized darkening along the binding margin, consistent with sewing stations or binding thread marks.
items.scholarly_note: Standard feature of medieval codex construction; no active structural damage.

items.description: Isolated pinpoint dark speck (approx. 0.5–1 mm), possibly flyspeck or incidental pigment.
items.scholarly_note: Non-deliberate; common in paper/parchment manuscripts of this period.

items.description: Very faint low-contrast circular mark, barely visible against the paper.
items.scholarly_note: Incidental dirt or paper inclusion; not ink.

scratches: Faint vertical linear impression running parallel to the left edge, approximately 3–4 cm from the gutter; likely a paper crease or manufacturing impression rather than a deliberate scratch.
damage_notes: Bottom-left corner exhibits minor fraying with exposed paper fibers.
Left inner margin shows typical binding-related punctures and localized discoloration.
summary: Page 8 of Codex Regius is exceptionally clean with no visible marginalia, doodles, pen trials, scribal corrections, or deliberate marks. The only features are minor binding-related damage in the gutter, two tiny incidental specks, and slight corner abrasion at the bottom left. The writing surface itself appears blank in this image.
